title: Mouroucoa panamensis
taxonomy: Mouroucoa panamensis is classified in the family Rubiaceae. This plant belongs to the genus Mouroucoa, which is a small genus of flowering plants. The species Mouroucoa panamensis is native to Panama and is found in the tropical rainforests of the region. It is a type of shrub or small tree that grows up to a certain height, and its classification helps scientists understand its evolutionary relationships with other plants. Further research is needed to fully understand the classification and taxonomy of Mouroucoa panamensis.
uses: Mouroucoa panamensis has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. Its bark is used to treat fever and rheumatism. In some regions, its leaves are used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and cough. Additionally, Mouroucoa panamensis is also used to treat various gastrointestinal issues. However, more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness for these medicinal uses.
